meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. An advanced case study leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid development.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for clients to build a portfolio. Use Claude Code to rapidly scaffold high-fidelity, production-ready modules, then use Antigravity to automate your Git commit history. By transforming complex features into structured, documented Git repositories, you create an undeniable 'proof of work' that demonstrates senior-level architectural thinking to prospective clients.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a freelancer's greatest enemy, but the 'empty repository' is the silent killer of your career. You’ve got the skills, but without a visible trail of commits, your professional credibility remains theoretical. Clients don't buy code; they buy the confidence that you’ve solved their specific problem before.

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of building toy apps, you will leverage AI-driven workflows to generate, document, and deploy enterprise-grade components. By integrating Claude Code’s reasoning with Antigravity’s deployment automation, you aren't just writing code—you are manufacturing a high-velocity engineering reputation in real-time.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Module
steps.description: Identify a complex, repeatable engineering challenge (e.g., a custom authentication middleware or a data-processing pipeline) to serve as your portfolio cornerstone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the requirements for a high-concurrency Node.js event bus. Create a modular structure that prioritizes scalability and clean error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold a robust event bus architecture using TypeScript and Zod for validation'

steps.title: Rapid Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to flesh out the business logic. Focus on writing self-documenting code that reflects senior-level patterns like Dependency Injection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the core logic for the event bus, ensuring thread-safety and comprehensive JSDoc comments for all exported interfaces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'implement core event bus architecture with full JSDoc coverage'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suite
steps.description: A portfolio without tests is a liability. Automate the generation of unit and integration tests to prove your commitment to code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that covers 90% edge-case scenarios, including race conditions in the event bus.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate Jest unit tests for event bus with 90% coverage target'

steps.title: Git History Curation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to ensure your commit history tells a professional story. Clean, atomic commits are the mark of a seasoned developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the git history into atomic, descriptive commits that document the evolution of the project architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: git rebase -i HEAD~n (use Claude to squash and format messages)

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finalize the repository with a professional README.md that explains the 'Why' behind your architectural decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README that outlines the system architecture, installation instructions, and the engineering rationale behind the design choices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a professional README.md with architecture diagrams and usage examples'

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits; one feature per commit makes you look like a seasoned team player.
Include a 'Performance Benchmark' section in your README to show you care about production metrics.
Use Claude Code to explain code in the PR comments; clients check PRs to see how you communicate.
Focus on 'Hard Problems' like state management or security, not basic UI components.
Tag your releases using Semantic Versioning to show you understand release lifecycles.
content.next_steps: After completing your first module, wrap it into a reusable NPM package or Docker container and publish it to show you can ship end-to-end solutions.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting a lack of history hold back your freelance rates. By using these tools, you build a library of proven, high-quality code that acts as a 24/7 sales engine for your business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
